mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable consumer prices indicates robust household purchasing power without inflationary pressure, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 1.5 percentage points annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of monetary tightening reflects resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs higher financing costs, yielding positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01 or positive return frequency drops below 45 percent during payroll expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500 thousand units signals a collapse in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of months where HOUST remains below the 500 thousand threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside stagnant federal funds rates indicate eroding real yields and delayed monetary policy response, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a positive predictive IC when CPIAUCSL increases by greater than 0.5 percent monthly while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms economic expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of recovery period observations.
